title: Lift json.ts/toml.ts user-facing config writes to atomic
description: Per Q2 grill-me decision: T-R7.6 must complete BEFORE T-MCP-CONFIG-PATH (R5) — Claude config rewrite (TASK-017) builds on atomic writes. config/json.ts:59 writes user-facing client MCP configs (currently raw fs.writeFile); corruption bricks the user's AI client. config/toml.ts:115 same for Codex TOML. Lift both to atomicWriteText/atomicWriteJson from shared. HIGHEST-priority P1 atomic migration. After this lands, TASK-017 only needs to focus on path logic.
scope: Layer 2 P0 (priority-elevated from P1) — must precede TASK-017

files.change: Line 59: replace raw fs.writeFile with atomicWriteJson from shared. Add tests to ensure existing config writers still emit identical content.
Line 115: replace raw fs.writeFile with atomicWriteText (TOML serialized first) from shared.
NEW: tests asserting (a) no .tmp file leftover after success, (b) target file not present until rename completes, (c) crash mid-write leaves .tmp not target

criteria: json.ts and toml.ts writes go through shared atomicWriteText/atomicWriteJson
Existing client config writers produce byte-identical output (no formatting drift)
Atomic guarantee tests pass (tmp + rename verified)
